Monday: Margaret opened with "Jalapeño corn dog bites. We're doing it this week and I need to know right..." - questioning whether the dish had real technique behind it. Marcus confirmed the cornmeal structure and jalapeño heat control were genuinely skilled. Stephanie proposed leading with the moisture-seal angle; Margaret approved, and Marcus was assigned to anchor copy around that concept.

Tuesday: Margaret confirmed a 60-40 cornmeal-to-flour minimum after testing - "Marcus gets it. Fine. Cornmeal-to-flour ratio is 60-40 minimum or the seal breaks down in the oil." Marcus framed this as a performance threshold, not a limitation. Stephanie agreed the copy should lead with performance over ingredients. All three aligned - product is moving forward.

Wednesday: Julian presented three shots of the bite-through moment. Stephanie pushed for shot two - the crisp exterior shattering - saying "Shot two's the one - if people are stopping their scroll, it's because they see that crisp exterior breaking apart, right?" Margaret agreed, prioritizing the crisp over Julian's cross-section shot. Shot two was locked in.

Thursday: Marcus brought finalized copy focusing on cornmeal's moisture-seal function. Margaret pushed to lead with the steam-absorption mechanic - not taste. Stephanie flagged repetition: "Love that you went all in on the moisture-seal thing - let's see what you've got?" Marcus tightened the structure to lead with cornmeal arresting moisture, then pivot to texture. Margaret approved - cut the explanation, show the fry result.

Friday: Margaret kicked off: "Final review. Shot two locked, copy locked. Someone tell me what isn't right, because something..." - but the team had no notes. Marcus credited trusting the work over overthinking it. Stephanie noted the team listened instead of defending positions. Julian and Marcus agreed restraint drove both the shot and the copy.

Saturday: This conversation contains no discussion of "Jalapeno Corn Dog Bites" - it is entirely about a website deployment. Devon confirmed verification passed and pushed the site live. Margaret commented that restraint leads to cleaner results, which Devon agreed with. No food ingredients or techniques were discussed.
